proof_boundary: This artifact proves uniqueness and an exact characterization of any integer-only multiplier counting measure. It does not construct that measure, identify the natural Mellin interpolation with the canonical Hausdorff interpolation, prove PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda <= 0.

rows.claim: Each elementary multiplier curvature is a positive Laplace transform.
A convergent unit counting multiset defines a finite positive Laplace density.
The integer log contractions are Hausdorff moments of one finite measure.
rows.proof_boundary: Change of variables r=exp(-t); total mass is y_1.

rows.claim: All integer values y_1,y_2,... uniquely determine nu.
rows.formula: int r^n dnu=int r^n dnu_tilde for all n>=0 implies nu=nu_tilde
rows.proof_boundary: Polynomial density in C([0,1]) and finite-measure duality.

rows.claim: Hausdorff positivity gives every alternating finite-difference inequality.
rows.proof_boundary: Necessary for the counting product, not sufficient for unit atoms.

rows.claim: The integer product target is equivalent to a rigid property of the unique Hausdorff measure.
rows.formula: dnu/dr=q(-log r)*sum_j r^alpha_j with unit integer multiplicity and sum_j alpha_j^(-2)<infinity
rows.proof_boundary: Characterization only; existence of the density and atoms is open.

rows.claim: If the counting representation exists, its multiset is unique.
rows.formula: S(t)=(dnu/dr)(exp(-t))/q(t); uniqueness of Laplace transforms determines sum_j delta_(alpha_j)
rows.proof_boundary: Uniqueness does not supply existence or an extraction algorithm.

rows.claim: The available zeta sequence passes the full finite Hausdorff sign frontier.
rows.formula: 7980/7980 strict signs through order 55 on five heat parameters
rows.proof_boundary: Finite interval evidence only; not a representing-measure theorem.

rows.claim: Integer agreement and even continuous curvature agreement do not identify the natural Mellin interpolation.
rows.proof_boundary: A growth or canonical-interpolation theorem is still required before using the continuous Mellin power-sum obstruction.

rows.claim: Recover or rule out the unique deconvolved unit counting measure.
rows.formula: nu -> h(r)=dnu/dr -> S(t)=h(exp(-t))/q(t) -> unit counting measure N
rows.proof_boundary: This recovery is the remaining multiplier target, not a proved step.

status: exact Hausdorff uniqueness and unit-atomic characterization
